issues.description: FR-2a/b/c/f: architecture.md uses numbered headings (## 1. System Architecture Overview, ## 2. ..., up to ## 9.). The spec instructs adding new ## sections (Judge, Dashboard, Pluggable cache, Observability) after ## 4 without addressing the numbering scheme. Implementer must decide: (a) number the new sections 5-8 and renumber existing 5-9 to 9-13, or (b) leave them unnumbered, creating inconsistency. This is ambiguous enough to produce divergent implementations.
issues.suggestion: State explicitly whether to continue the numbering scheme (and renumber subsequent sections) or drop the numbering convention entirely from the file.

issues.description:
FR-1d: The spec says 'add a

inside the .container div, after the .arch-flow div, before the closing of the container'. However, there is already a

at line 114 between the .arch-flow div and the closing . The instruction is ambiguous: should the new

go before or after the existing arch-tagline paragraph?
issues.suggestion:
Clarify placement relative to the existing

. Likely intent: insert the new

between the arch-tagline and the closing , or between .arch-flow and arch-tagline.

issues.description: FR-2e: The existing state machine diagram already handles AWAITING_REAUTH generically with 'ANY active state ----[creds expired]----> AWAITING_REAUTH' (lines 442-449). The spec asks to add explicit per-state transitions (IMPLEMENTING -> AWAITING_REAUTH, REVIEWING -> AWAITING_REAUTH, HARDENING -> AWAITING_REAUTH) which duplicate the existing generic rule. It's unclear whether the intent is to replace the generic notation with per-state arrows, or add per-state arrows alongside the generic notation.
issues.suggestion: Clarify: should the existing 'ANY active state -> AWAITING_REAUTH' generic notation be replaced with per-state arrows, left as-is with the per-state arrows added redundantly, or should only the new FAILED -> {failed_from_state} transition be added (since AWAITING_REAUTH is already covered)?

issues.description:
FR-1e refers to the '331 production bugs' line as 'bold' but the actual markup is

331 production bugs caught by cross-model review before first deploy.

— it uses an amber-colored span, not . Minor, but an implementer reading 'retain the bold line' might look for a tag.
issues.suggestion:
Replace 'bold' with 'callout' or reference the class name: 'Retain the existing

line'.

issues.description: Baseline section says www/index.html is 342 lines; actual file is 343 lines. docs/architecture.md is stated as 1141 lines, actual is 1142. Minor inaccuracies that don't affect implementation.
issues.suggestion: Correct line counts if updating the spec, but this doesn't block implementation.

issues.description: FR-1e: The replacement table changes from 3 columns (Lane, Rounds, Findings) to 4 columns (Phase, What, Output, Notes). The spec provides table content but doesn't explicitly mention replacing the <thead>. An implementer would figure this out, but it's an implicit requirement.
issues.suggestion: Add a note that the entire <table> structure (including <thead>) should be replaced, not just the <tbody> rows.

issues.description:
FR-1e: The new v0.6.0 callout line ('v0.6.0: nautiloop shipped 12+ of its own feature PRs in a single day of dogfooding.') doesn't specify what CSS class or HTML structure to use. The existing callout uses

with . Should the new line use the same class/pattern?
issues.suggestion:
Specify: 'Add a sibling

using the same markup pattern as the existing 331 callout line.'
